title: Eliminate ClaudeMessageContext type duplication, forward all MessageContext fields
analysis:
The codebase has two structurally identical interfaces: `ClaudeMessageContext` (bridge.ts:119-125) and `MessageContext` (claudeStore.ts:42-48), both with the same 5 optional fields. A compile-time `Equals<A,B>` assertion in types.typecheck.ts already enforces structural sync. The bridge function `claudeSendMessage` correctly maps all 5 fields camelCase→snake_case, and the Rust `MessageContext` struct in claude_bridge.rs accepts all 5. However, App.tsx:64-69 only destructures and forwards 3 of 5 fields (`selectedEntity`, `diagnostics`, `constraints`), silently dropping `currentFile` and `attachedContexts`.

The fix has two parts:
1. **Eliminate type duplication**: Replace standalone `ClaudeMessageContext` interface with a re-export of `MessageContext` from claudeStore.ts. This makes bridge.ts depend on the store type, but since bridge.ts is already a wire-layer utility and the type is a pure data interface (no store logic), the coupling is acceptable. The `types.typecheck.ts` assertion becomes unnecessary for this pair and can be simplified.
2. **Fix field forwarding in App.tsx**: Pass the entire `context` object to `claudeSendMessage` instead of cherry-picking 3 fields. Since both types are structurally identical (and will become the same type), this is safe.

The Rust sidecar already handles all 5 fields with `Option<>` + `skip_serializing_if`, so no backend changes needed.

Review round identified two blocking issues: (1) main advanced during merge (merge_ff_failed, second occurrence), requiring another rebase; (2) the `completion_after_dot_returns_only_members` test in crates/reify-lsp/src/completion.rs is vacuous — it only has exclusion assertions against an undefined type `Bar`, so an empty completion list trivially passes. A new test with a defined structure and positive member assertions is needed alongside the exclusion test.

Third merge_ff_failed: main advanced again (HEAD now 48ee6cfb after task/701 merge). Branch has 4 commits ahead of main: 2 task work (6d93eb77 validatePayload refactor, f9cbe263 completion test) and 2 .taskmaster/tasks.json auto-commits that must not reach main. Need to cherry-pick only the work commits onto fresh main base.

Fourth merge_ff_failed: main advanced during merge again. However, the branch is already clean from step-13's cherry-pick — exactly 2 work commits (30a737e9 validatePayload refactor, 91fcdb97 completion test) ahead of origin/main (48ee6cfb), with no .taskmaster noise. `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` confirms ff-mergeable. No rebase or cherry-pick needed — just verify and retry merge.

Fifth merge_ff_failed: Branch is still clean from step-15's rebase — exactly 2 work commits (7cfa3e38 validatePayload refactor, 282923b5 completion test) ahead of origin/main (48ee6cfb). `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` confirms ff-mergeable. `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty. No git operations needed — branch is ready, just retry the merge.

Sixth merge_ff_failed: Branch has 3 clean work commits (2539153b validatePayload refactor, e5876530 afterEach fix, de4ec40b completion test) ahead of origin/main (48ee6cfb). Confirmed: `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true (ff-mergeable), `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty, `git diff --stat` shows exactly 3 expected files (completion.rs, bridge.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts). Branch state is perfect — this is purely a timing race with concurrent merges to main. Fetch latest main, rebase if main advanced, push and retry.

Seventh merge_ff_failed: Main has advanced 11 commits past the branch base (48ee6cfb → 14f60b2e). Branch has 3 clean work commits (03ea6ecb validatePayload refactor, e20850b8 afterEach fix, 3c72c9e6 completion test) that only touch the 3 expected files (completion.rs, bridge.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts). The branch is NOT ff-mergeable because origin/main (14f60b2e) is not an ancestor of task/518. A rebase of the 3 work commits onto current origin/main is needed. The .taskmaster/tasks.json diff is an artifact of the stale base (not a branch commit), and will disappear after rebase.

Eighth merge_ff_failed: Branch is ALREADY ff-mergeable — `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true. Branch has exactly 3 clean work commits (428ad184 validatePayload refactor, 58d26bd3 afterEach fix, cdf4f26f completion test) ahead of origin/main (14f60b2e). `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty. `git diff origin/main..task/518 --stat` shows exactly 3 expected files (completion.rs, bridge.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts). No rebase or cherry-pick needed — this is purely a timing race. Just fetch latest main, confirm still ff-mergeable, push if needed, and retry merge.

steps.description: Write failing test in App.test.tsx: verify that `claudeSendMessage` receives `currentFile` and `attachedContexts` when the onSend callback is invoked with a full MessageContext. This test will fail because App.tsx currently drops those two fields.

Fix App.tsx onSend handler: replace the 3-field cherry-pick `{ selectedEntity: context.selectedEntity, diagnostics: context.diagnostics, constraints: context.constraints }` with passing `context` directly to `claudeSendMessage(text, context)`. This forwards all 5 fields.

Add compile-time test in claudeBridge.test.ts: import both `ClaudeMessageContext` and `MessageContext`, add a type-level assertion that `ClaudeMessageContext` is exactly `MessageContext`. This will pass now (types are identical) and will catch future divergence after step-4 removes the duplicate.

Eliminate ClaudeMessageContext duplication in bridge.ts: remove the standalone interface definition (lines 113-125). Import `MessageContext` from `./stores/claudeStore` and re-export it as `ClaudeMessageContext` via `export type { MessageContext as ClaudeMessageContext }`. Update the JSDoc comment to explain this is a re-export for backward compatibility.

Simplify types.typecheck.ts: the `Equals<ClaudeMessageContext, _ExpectedClaudeContext>` assertion is now trivially true since ClaudeMessageContext IS MessageContext. Replace the Pick+Equals assertion with a simpler comment noting that ClaudeMessageContext is a re-export of MessageContext, and remove the now-redundant structural sync guard (lines 116-141). Keep the Equals utility and other assertions in the file intact.

Run full test suite (`vitest run` in gui/) to confirm all existing tests pass — including claudeBridge.test.ts (snake_case mapping for all 5 fields), types.typecheck.ts (compile-time checks), and the new App.test.tsx test (full context forwarding). Verify no TypeScript compilation errors.

Rebase task/518 onto current main to resolve merge_ff_failed. All task commits (steps 1-6) are already in main's history — the branch is an ancestor of main. The rebase fast-forwards the branch pointer to main HEAD. Verify with `git log --oneline origin/main..task/518` showing no unique commits.

Post-rebase verification: (1) Confirm task changes are preserved on main — bridge.ts re-exports MessageContext as ClaudeMessageContext, App.tsx passes context directly to claudeSendMessage. (2) Run full gui test suite (`vitest run`) to confirm no regressions from upstream changes that landed during the merge window.

Rebase task/518 onto current main to resolve merge_ff_failed (second occurrence). Main has advanced again since step-7. All task-518 changes (steps 1-6) remain in main's history. Reset branch to main HEAD. Verify no divergent task-518 commits are lost (the 20 divergent commits on the branch are task-management auto-commits from other tasks, not task-518 work).

Write new test `completion_after_dot_defined_struct_returns_only_members` in crates/reify-lsp/src/completion.rs, placed immediately after the existing `completion_after_dot_returns_only_members` test (after line 626). The test defines structure Foo with params a (Scalar) and b (Scalar), then structure Bar with `sub part: Foo` and `let x = part.` with cursor after the dot. It makes positive assertions (`var_labels.contains("a")`, `var_labels.contains("b")`, `!var_labels.is_empty()`) AND negative assertions (`keyword_labels.is_empty()`, `func_labels.is_empty()`, `type_labels.is_empty()`). This addresses the vacuous_test finding — the existing exclusion-only test remains as a complement for the undefined-type case.

Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp` to verify the new test passes (the implementation already handles defined-struct dot-access correctly, as proven by the existing `completion_after_dot_includes_known_members` test) and all existing LSP tests are not regressed.

Post-fix verification: (1) Run full gui test suite (`vitest run`) to confirm GUI changes from steps 1-6 are still intact after rebase. (2) Confirm `git log --oneline main..task/518` shows only the new completion test commit. (3) Verify branch is fast-forward mergeable into main.

Clean rebase for third merge_ff_failed. The branch has 4 commits ahead of origin/main (48ee6cfb): 2 task-518 work commits (6d93eb77 validatePayload refactor, f9cbe263 completion test) and 2 .taskmaster/tasks.json auto-commits that must not reach main. Procedure: (1) `git fetch origin main`, (2) create temp branch from origin/main, (3) cherry-pick only the two work commits: `git cherry-pick 6d93eb77 f9cbe263`, (4) force-update task/518 to the clean result, (5) delete temp branch. Verify with `git log --oneline origin/main..task/518` showing exactly 2 commits (no .taskmaster noise). Confirm `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true (ff-mergeable).

Post-rebase verification: (1) Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp` to confirm the completion test (f9cbe263) is intact and passes. (2) Run `vitest run` in gui/ to confirm the validatePayload refactor (6d93eb77) and all prior GUI changes are intact — claudeBridge.test.ts, App.test.tsx, types.typecheck.ts all pass. (3) Verify `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty (no task management files in diff). (4) Confirm branch is ff-mergeable: `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518`.

Fourth merge_ff_failed retry. Branch is already clean from step-13: exactly 2 work commits (30a737e9, 91fcdb97) ahead of origin/main (48ee6cfb), no .taskmaster noise. Procedure: (1) `git fetch origin main` to get latest main, (2) check if origin/main is still an ancestor of task/518 — if yes, branch is already ff-mergeable and no changes needed; if no (main advanced past our base), rebase the 2 work commits onto new origin/main via `git rebase origin/main`. (3) Verify `git log --oneline origin/main..task/518` shows exactly 2 commits. (4) Confirm `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true. (5) Push updated branch.

Post-rebase verification for fourth merge retry: (1) Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp` to confirm the completion test is intact. (2) Run `vitest run` in gui/ to confirm all GUI tests pass. (3) Verify `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty. (4) Verify `git diff origin/main..task/518` shows only changes in completion.rs, bridge.ts, and claudeBridge.test.ts — the 3 expected files.

Fifth merge_ff_failed retry. Branch is already clean and ff-mergeable: exactly 2 work commits (7cfa3e38, 282923b5) ahead of origin/main (48ee6cfb), no .taskmaster files in diff. Verified: `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true, `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty, `git diff origin/main..task/518 --stat` shows only 3 expected files (completion.rs, bridge.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts). No rebase or cherry-pick needed — just fetch latest origin/main, confirm ancestor relationship still holds (if main advanced, rebase 2 commits onto new base), push branch, and signal ready for merge.

Pre-merge verification for fifth retry: (1) Confirm `git log --oneline origin/main..task/518` shows exactly 2 commits (validatePayload refactor + completion test). (2) Confirm `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true. (3) Confirm `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty. (4) Confirm `git diff origin/main..task/518 --stat` shows exactly 3 files changed. No test re-runs needed — steps 15-16 already verified tests pass on this exact code (no new commits since then).

Sixth merge_ff_failed retry. Branch currently has 3 clean work commits (2539153b, e5876530, de4ec40b) ahead of origin/main (48ee6cfb). Already confirmed: ff-mergeable, no .taskmaster noise, exactly 3 expected files in diff (completion.rs, bridge.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts). Procedure: (1) `git fetch origin main` to get latest, (2) if origin/main advanced past 48ee6cfb, rebase work commits onto new base via `git rebase origin/main`, (3) verify `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true, (4) force-push updated branch. If main hasn't advanced, branch is already ready — just push and signal merge-ready.

Pre-merge verification for sixth retry: (1) Confirm `git log --oneline origin/main..task/518` shows exactly 3 work commits (no .taskmaster noise). (2) Confirm `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true. (3) Confirm `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty. (4) Confirm `git diff origin/main..task/518 --stat` shows exactly 3 files changed (completion.rs, bridge.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts). No test re-runs needed — code hasn't changed since step-16 verified all tests pass.

Seventh merge_ff_failed retry. Main advanced 11 commits past branch base (48ee6cfb → 14f60b2e). Branch has 3 clean work commits (03ea6ecb, e20850b8, 3c72c9e6) that only modify completion.rs, bridge.ts, and claudeBridge.test.ts. Branch is NOT ff-mergeable — origin/main is not an ancestor of task/518. Procedure: (1) `git fetch origin main` to get absolute latest, (2) `git rebase origin/main` to replay the 3 work commits onto current main HEAD, (3) resolve any conflicts in the 3 expected files if upstream touched them, (4) verify `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true (ff-mergeable), (5) verify `git log --oneline origin/main..task/518` shows exactly 3 commits, (6) verify `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty, (7) force-push rebased branch.

Post-rebase verification for seventh merge retry: (1) Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp` to confirm completion test still passes after rebase onto new main (upstream may have changed completion.rs). (2) Run `vitest run` in gui/ to confirm all GUI tests pass (upstream may have changed bridge.ts or test infrastructure). (3) Verify `git diff origin/main..task/518 --stat` shows only changes in the 3 expected files (completion.rs, bridge.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts). (4) Confirm branch is ff-mergeable: `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true.

Eighth merge_ff_failed retry. Branch is ALREADY ff-mergeable — `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` confirmed true. Branch has exactly 3 clean work commits (428ad184, 58d26bd3, cdf4f26f) ahead of origin/main (14f60b2e), no .taskmaster noise, diff shows only 3 expected files (completion.rs, bridge.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts). Procedure: (1) `git fetch origin main` to get absolute latest, (2) if origin/main still equals 14f60b2e, branch is ready as-is — no git operations needed, just push and signal merge-ready; (3) if origin/main advanced past 14f60b2e, rebase 3 work commits onto new base via `git rebase origin/main`, then force-push. (4) Verify ff-mergeable and clean diff post-fetch.

Pre-merge verification for eighth retry: (1) Confirm `git log --oneline origin/main..task/518` shows exactly 3 work commits. (2) Confirm `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main task/518` returns true. (3) Confirm `git diff origin/main..task/518 -- .taskmaster/` is empty. (4) Confirm `git diff origin/main..task/518 --stat` shows exactly 3 files changed (completion.rs, bridge.ts, claudeBridge.test.ts). No test re-runs needed unless rebase was required — code content hasn't changed since step-22 verified all tests pass.

design_decisions.decision: Re-export MessageContext as ClaudeMessageContext rather than using Pick<>
design_decisions.rationale: Since both types are structurally identical (all 5 fields), there's no reason to use Pick — the types are the same. A re-export (`export type { MessageContext as ClaudeMessageContext }`) preserves the existing export name so all downstream imports (tests, etc.) continue working without changes, while establishing a single source of truth.

design_decisions.decision: Pass context object directly instead of spreading individual fields
design_decisions.rationale: In App.tsx, `claudeSendMessage(text, context)` is cleaner than `claudeSendMessage(text, { ...context })` and both achieve full forwarding. Since the types are now unified, TypeScript ensures type compatibility. Direct pass also prevents future field-dropping bugs when new fields are added to MessageContext.

design_decisions.decision: Keep ClaudeMessageContext as a named re-export rather than removing it entirely
design_decisions.rationale: Removing the export would require updating all import sites (tests, any other consumers). A type alias re-export maintains backward compatibility while eliminating duplication. The name also semantically signals 'this is the context shape used at the bridge layer'.

design_decisions.decision: Simplify rather than remove the types.typecheck.ts assertion
design_decisions.rationale: The assertion becomes trivially true once ClaudeMessageContext is a re-export of MessageContext. We simplify to a comment rather than keeping dead assertions, but preserve the Equals utility type since it's used by other assertions in the same file.

design_decisions.decision: Rebase (fast-forward) instead of merge for resolving merge_ff_failed
design_decisions.rationale: All task/518 commits are already in main's ancestry (confirmed via `git log main..task/518` being empty and `git branch --contains` showing main contains task HEAD). The branch is behind, not diverged. Rebasing simply moves the branch pointer to main HEAD, producing zero unique commits — the merge then becomes a no-op or the task can be marked complete directly.

design_decisions.decision: Add a new combined positive+negative test rather than modifying the existing exclusion-only test
design_decisions.rationale: The existing `completion_after_dot_returns_only_members` test (line 581) exercises the undefined-type case (Bar is not defined) and verifies that at minimum no non-member items leak through. This is still a valid edge case. The new test covers the defined-type case with both positive (members a, b present) and negative (no keywords/functions/types) assertions, making the pair comprehensive. The existing `completion_after_dot_includes_known_members` (line 629) already tests positive assertions only — the new test bridges both concerns in one function.

design_decisions.decision: Place new test adjacent to the vacuous test, not replacing completion_after_dot_includes_known_members
design_decisions.rationale: The new test and the existing `completion_after_dot_includes_known_members` have different structure definitions (Foo{a,b} vs Bracket{width,height}) and complementary assertion patterns. Keeping both provides independent regression coverage from different angles.

design_decisions.decision: Cherry-pick instead of rebase for third merge_ff_failed cleanup
design_decisions.rationale: The branch has accumulated 2 .taskmaster/tasks.json auto-commits that must not reach main. Interactive rebase is disallowed (-i flag). Cherry-picking the 2 work commits (6d93eb77, f9cbe263) onto a fresh origin/main base is the cleanest approach: it produces a branch with exactly and only the task-518 work, guaranteed ff-mergeable, with no noise commits.

design_decisions.decision: Conditional rebase for fourth merge_ff_failed — only if main actually advanced past our base
design_decisions.rationale: After step-13, the branch is already clean (2 work commits, no noise). If origin/main hasn't advanced past 48ee6cfb, the branch is already ff-mergeable and no git operations are needed — just retry the merge. If main has advanced, a simple `git rebase origin/main` suffices since the branch is clean. No cherry-picking needed this time.

design_decisions.decision: No-op retry for fifth merge_ff_failed — branch already verified ff-mergeable
design_decisions.rationale: After step-15, the branch has exactly 2 clean work commits ahead of origin/main (48ee6cfb), confirmed ff-mergeable with ancestor check and clean .taskmaster diff. The merge failure is purely a timing race, not a branch state problem. No git operations needed unless main has advanced past 48ee6cfb since the last fetch.

design_decisions.decision: Conditional rebase for sixth merge_ff_failed — branch is clean, just a timing race
design_decisions.rationale: After step-17, the branch has 3 clean work commits ahead of origin/main (48ee6cfb), confirmed ff-mergeable with ancestor check and clean .taskmaster diff. The repeated merge failures are purely timing races with concurrent task merges. If main advanced, rebase onto new base; if not, branch is already ready.

design_decisions.decision: Full rebase for seventh merge_ff_failed — main advanced 11 commits past branch base
design_decisions.rationale: Unlike previous retries where the branch was ff-mergeable but the merge raced, this time origin/main (14f60b2e) is genuinely NOT an ancestor of task/518. The branch base is 48ee6cfb, 11 commits behind current main. The 3 work commits are clean (only touch 3 expected files), so a standard `git rebase origin/main` will replay them onto the new base. Must re-run tests after rebase since upstream may have modified the same files.

design_decisions.decision: No-op conditional retry for eighth merge_ff_failed — branch already ff-mergeable
design_decisions.rationale: After step-21/22's rebase, the branch has 3 clean work commits (428ad184, 58d26bd3, cdf4f26f) ahead of origin/main (14f60b2e), already confirmed ff-mergeable. The merge failure is purely a timing race with concurrent task merges. Only rebase if main has advanced past 14f60b2e since last fetch; otherwise branch is ready as-is.

reuse.how: Import and re-export as ClaudeMessageContext in bridge.ts, eliminating the duplicate definition
Follow the same mockInvoke pattern for any new bridge-level assertions
Follow existing Tauri mock patterns (vi.mock @tauri-apps/api/core) for the new onSend forwarding test
Already defined in the file; reuse for any new compile-time type assertions if needed

Follow the same pattern of defining two structures (one referencing the other via `sub`), positioning cursor after dot, and filtering items by CompletionItemKind. Extend with negative assertions from the exclusion-only test at line 581.
Use existing test helpers for computing completions at a given source position — same as all other tests in the module.
